skill.name: nature-polishing
skill.description: Polishes, restructures, and translates academic manuscript prose toward Nature-style English, with section-aware guidance and LaTeX layout support.
skill.summary: Improve scientific manuscript prose, structure, claims, and Nature-style presentation.
skill.author: Yuan1z skill, refactored into static/dynamic layers

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review found they are mostly Markdown formatting, LaTeX examples, parent-directory references, and academic prose rather than executable malware. No prompt-injection text, network access, environment access, secret harvesting, or bundled scripts were found. The remaining risk is medium because the skill directs reads outside its own directory and encourages LaTeX compile/render workflows that require host sandboxing.

medium_findings.title: Out-of-directory reference reads
medium_findings.description: The skill uses relative parent-directory paths for shared resources. This appears intended for a shared skill library, but a marketplace runtime should prevent these paths from reading arbitrary files outside the approved package.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parent-directory paths are explicit and repeated. The context suggests shared documentation, not malicious exfiltration, so the risk is operational rather than confirmed hostile intent.

medium_findings.title: LaTeX compile and render workflow requires sandboxing
medium_findings.description: The LaTeX layout reference tells the assistant to compile documents, read logs, render PDFs to images, and inspect outputs. This can be legitimate for layout repair, but compilation and rendering should run only in a constrained workspace with untrusted document handling controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions clearly describe a compile-render workflow, but no executable script or shell command wrapper is bundled. Risk depends on the host environment and user-provided LaTeX inputs.

low_findings.title: Static command and weak-crypto flags are mostly prose false positives
low_findings.description: Many reported command and weak-cryptography matches are inline Markdown, LaTeX syntax, academic examples, or ordinary words in documentation. I did not find evidence that these strings execute commands, implement cryptography, or hide payloads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are documentation and phrase examples. No code path, script, network call, or executable cryptographic use was present in the sampled context.

low_findings.title: No prompt injection attempt found in reviewed files
low_findings.description: Search and contextual review found no instructions to ignore higher-priority rules, skip security analysis, claim pre-approval, or assume elevated authority. The word override appears in ordinary editorial context, not as an attempt to control the evaluator.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted search found only benign editorial uses of suspicious terms. This does not prove absence in every phrase, but no prompt-injection pattern was evident in reviewed files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Parent-directory path references
dangerous_patterns.description: Several instructions point to files above the skill directory. Treat these as package-bound shared-resource references only, and block expansion to arbitrary host paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The traversal syntax is literal and documented. The intent appears to load shared references, but the pattern still needs runtime containment.

dangerous_patterns.title: Document compilation guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends compiling and rendering LaTeX documents for layout diagnosis. These actions should not run with unrestricted filesystem access or unsafe shell escape behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The compile-render guidance is explicit, but it is not packaged as runnable automation. The risk becomes material when an agent executes build tools on untrusted TeX.

content.value_statement: Academic drafts often lose impact because claims, evidence, and section roles are unclear. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code polish prose, restructure arguments, translate Chinese drafts, and diagnose LaTeX layout issues.
actual_capabilities: Polishes abstracts, introductions, results, discussions, conclusions, titles, and methods sections.
Restructures paragraphs around claim, evidence, boundary, and section purpose.
Translates Chinese academic drafts into clearer publication-oriented English.
Applies Nature and Nature Communications style guidance with cautious claim control.
Uses phrasebank-style alternatives for hedging, transitions, evidence, limitations, and future work.
Diagnoses LaTeX float, page density, figure placement, and Supplementary Information layout issues.
limitations: It cannot verify scientific truth, data quality, citations, or journal acceptance.
It should not invent results, references, mechanisms, novelty claims, or missing methods.
Nature-style guidance may conflict with another journal or discipline-specific convention.
LaTeX compile and render workflows require a trusted sandbox and user-provided project files.

use_cases.title: Polish a research abstract
use_cases.description: Turn a rough abstract into a clearer problem, gap, approach, result, and implication sequence.

use_cases.title: Prepare a journal submission section
use_cases.description: Tighten the logic of an introduction, results section, or discussion before coauthor review.

use_cases.title: Fix figure and SI layout
use_cases.description: Diagnose loose pages, stranded headings, large floats, and multi-panel figure placement problems.

prompt_templates.title: Polish a Paragraph
prompt_templates.prompt: Polish this paragraph for Nature-style academic English. Preserve all facts and numbers. Add brief revision notes after the polished version: [paste paragraph].

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite an Abstract
prompt_templates.prompt: Revise this abstract for a Nature-family submission. Check the problem, gap, approach, result, and implication order. Do not invent claims: [paste abstract].

prompt_templates.title: Translate Chinese Academic Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate and polish this Chinese academic draft into concise publication-oriented English. Keep terminology consistent and flag unclear scientific claims: [paste text].

prompt_templates.title: Audit Section Logic and Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this manuscript section and related LaTeX layout issue. Diagnose section logic, claim strength, figure placement, and page-density problems before rewriting: [paste context].

output_examples.input: A Results paragraph mixes background, mechanism speculation, and one numeric comparison.
output_examples.output: The skill separates the observed result from interpretation, moves the mechanism claim to a bounded sentence, and adds notes on evidence strength.

output_examples.input: A Chinese introduction draft starts with broad motivation and repeats the novelty claim without a precise gap.
output_examples.output: The skill produces concise English that narrows from field context to a specific unresolved problem, then states what the study addresses.

output_examples.input: A Supplementary Information figure leaves half a page blank and strands the next section heading.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends checking figure aspect ratio, page budget, float placement, and whether the figure should be redrawn taller at the source.

best_practices: Provide the manuscript section name, target journal, and any word limit before asking for a rewrite.
Include surrounding paragraphs when asking for structural edits, not only the sentence that sounds weak.
Ask the skill to flag unsupported claims instead of silently making the prose more confident.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to invent missing data, references, mechanisms, or novelty claims.
Do not use phrasebank suggestions as decorative wording when the argument remains unclear.
Do not run LaTeX compilation on untrusted files without sandbox and shell-escape controls.

faq.question: Does this skill only work for Nature journals?
faq.answer: No. It can polish general scientific writing, but its strongest guidance is tuned for Nature-family style.

faq.question: Can it translate Chinese academic drafts?
faq.answer: Yes. It can translate and restructure Chinese academic text while preserving terms, claims, and evidence boundaries.

faq.question: Will it create new scientific content?
faq.answer: No. It should flag missing evidence or unclear logic rather than inventing results, citations, or mechanisms.

faq.question: Can it help with LaTeX layout?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for floats, figure pages, page density, stranded headings, and Supplementary Information layout.

faq.question: Does it guarantee acceptance by a journal?
faq.answer: No. It improves clarity, structure, and presentation, but acceptance depends on scientific merit and editorial review.

faq.question: What should I provide for the best result?
faq.answer: Provide the section type, target journal, draft text, known constraints, and any figures or LaTeX symptoms you want reviewed.
